Exegesis of Genesis 22:17 - stars and sand, gate imagery
Exegesis of Genesis 26:4 - blessing to nations
Land as sign/down payment, not ultimate promise
Stars as angelic beings governing creation
Connection to Deuteronomy 32:8 and divine council
Philo of Alexandria quote confirming stars as divine beings

Distinction between demonic possession and mental illness
Further clarification on possession vs. illness
Avoid binary thinking
New Testament reiterations: Matthew 19:28, Revelation 20:4, 24 elders, third of stars
Paul as prophet/mystic, not modern theologian
Key terms: sons of God, seed, heirs - basis for reading Paul
Galatians 3:26-29 exegesis: baptism makes us sons and heirs
Galatians 4:5-7 exegesis: adoption as sons, Spirit of sonship
Father Matthew Baker on adoption (huiothesia) and theosis
Romans 8:14-17 exegesis: adoption, heirs, glorification
Romans 8:19-23 exegesis: creation waits for sons of God, redemption of bodies
Romans 8:29 exegesis: predestined to be conformed to Christ's image
Caller question: when was this teaching lost? Response: preserved in Orthodox tradition
Further on loss: translation issues, Western debates
Caller question: sanctification vs. theosis
Response: theosis is sanctification, but more cosmic and participatory
Paul's ordo salutis: justification, sanctification, glorification are facets, not steps
I am saved, being saved, will be saved - all true simultaneously
Concluding quote from Fr. Matthew Baker: adoption, power against demons, fearlessness
Final comments: theosis as purpose of creation, experienced now
